title: The Top 25 Best Adapted Screenplay Winners So Far (Updated for 2026)
text: According to Awards Radar's latest ranking, Aaron Sorkin's screenplay for The Social Network tops the list of best adapted screenplay winners. The article notes that this category often the spot for prestige fare to shine and frequently rewards major Best Picture contenders. The 2026 list includes Paul Thomas Anderson's recent win for One Battle After Another, ranked at #19. Other notable recent winners featured include Peter Straughan for Conclave (#16), Cord Jefferson for American Fiction (honorable mention), and Sian Heder for CODA (honorable mention). The top five are rounded out by Ted Tally's The Silence of the Lambs (#5), Steven Zaillian's Schindler's List (#4), William Goldman's All the President's Men (#3), and The Epstein brothers' Casablanca (#2). Classic winners like To Kill a Mockingbird and One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest also rank highly on the list, demonstrating the enduring quality of literary adaptations in Oscar history.
